theme_and_archetype.description: Authority, clarity, function-first. The dashboard acts as a 'Control Room' for HR data with strong semantic highlights for churn risks.

headings.usage: Use tight tracking (tracking-tight) for large sizes. Max text-5xl for major headers. NEVER use Inter for headings.

body.usage: Use for all data tables, inputs, labels, and analytical text.

mono.usage: Use for IDs, specific data metrics, or technical readouts.

dashboard_grid.base: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 md:gap-6

component_strategies.cards: Cardless or strictly 1px border with no shadow (border-border/40). Radius max `rounded-md` (6px). Use progressive disclosure logic.
component_strategies.kpi_widgets: Top-left alignment 'North Star' layout. Massive metric number, small subtitle, subtle trend indicator in semantic color.
component_strategies.buttons: Flat design. Primary: bg-primary text-primary-foreground. Secondary: border border-border bg-transparent hover:bg-secondary.
component_strategies.tables: Dense. Use `IBM Plex Sans`. Left-align text, right-align numbers. Sticky headers for long lists. Use standard pagination components.
component_strategies.badges: Pill-shaped (rounded-full) for Churn Risk. High Risk = Red bg/text, Med = Yellow, Low = Green. High-contrast border.
component_strategies.forms: Use Shadcn Form. Labels must be bold. Input fields need clear focus rings (`focus-visible:ring-primary focus-visible:ring-offset-2`).
component_strategies.charts: Recharts. Use semantic colors directly for the data series (e.g., High Risk bar = Red). Remove heavy grid lines, use dashed subtle lines for axes.
component_strategies.sidebar: Fixed, full-height. Must contain clear active states (bg-secondary/50) and bottom anchor for Dark Mode toggle and User Profile.
component_strategies.recommendations_panel: Use progressive disclosure. Show 'Retention Recommendations' as action-oriented text with distinct list styling.

interactions_and_motion.hover_states: All clickable elements must change background or border explicitly. Do not rely on opacity fading. e.g., hover:bg-slate-100 hover:text-black
interactions_and_motion.alerts: High Risk alerts should pulse subtly or use a harsh red border to command attention.

icons.usage: Use for sidebar nav, action buttons, alert indicators. Keep stroke width consistent (1.5 or 2).

login_background.description: Abstract architectural glass structure, use for login page split-screen background with `mix-blend-overlay` or subtle opacity.

employee_avatars.description: Professional smiling woman, use for seed data.
Man in suit, use for seed data.
Woman with arms crossed, use for seed data.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup Tailwind CSS to include the color_palette mapped to standard variables (e.g., bg-background, text-primary). Add dark mode via standard 'class' strategy.
2. Inject Google Fonts (Outfit, IBM Plex Sans, JetBrains Mono) into index.css and extend the Tailwind font-family theme accordingly.
3. Build the 'Control Room' grid layout for the Admin Dashboard exactly as specified: KPI cards col-span-1, main charts spanning multiple cols/rows, tight gaps (gap-4 or gap-6).
4. Use Semantic Colors directly in Recharts for Churn Risk visualizations (Red = High Risk, Yellow = Medium, Green = Low).
5. Aggressively remove 'card' drop-shadows from Shadcn components. Replace them with 1px borders (border-border/40) for a highly technical, Swiss aesthetic. Do not use border-radius larger than 6px.
6. Build a robust Sidebar Navigation layout with clear active states and a bottom section for user profile/theme toggle.
7. Construct the Employee Profile page using a dense 'Bento' style grid to display Attendance, Leaves, Performance Reviews, and Risk Score all in one view.
8. Implement an Alerts System feed specifically highlighting 'High Risk' indicators.

9. Every interactive element, nav link, and key text component MUST have a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid='login-button'`, `data-testid='employee-risk-badge'`, `data-testid='sidebar-nav-dashboard'`).
10. Write components using .js or .jsx (not TypeScript), conforming to standard React patterns.
